title: Thread-log's next era — the CLI-orchestrated thread system
charter: The evolution of thread-log from a Claude Code skill into a standalone, CLI-orchestrated thread-management system. In scope: design and construction of the core engine (a pure library that orchestrates subscription-harness agents — Claude Code / OpenCode — through the ingest pipeline), its interfaces (CLI first, MCP server / web API later over the same core), the SessionSource adapter port (Claude Code + OpenCode in v1, ChatGPT designed-for-but-deferred), storage/backup model (separate sensitive thread data, first-class backup destinations, two-way git sync, provenance/source-machine, local raw-session archival), observability (run registry, per-run logs, cost surfacing, model-action history), and the deferred-but-specced problem of higher-level thread structure (grouping vs. relationship graph vs. nothing). Builds on the thread-log-build thread (the skill being lifted into the system). Out of scope: ongoing hardening of the existing skill as a skill (thread-log-build / thread-log-usage cover that) and the domain work inside any thread the system manages.

sessions.title: Grilling session — design of the thread-log CLI/core system (this session's foundational design)
